title: pyrethrum polycephalum
intro: Pyrethrum polycephalum, also known as the feverfew plant, is a flowering perennial herb native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used medicinally for its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. The medicinal properties of pyrethrum polycephalum are attributed to the sesquiterpene lactones present in the plant, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-migraine effects, providing health benefits such as pain relief and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of pyrethrum polycephalum include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, including migraines, arthritis, and digestive issues.
